message:
fix(primer): correct jsm installer commands

QA pass for hgjp.34: fix the primer's CLI install snippet to use the official install.sh/install.ps1 endpoints instead of /install, add the reload note, and record the review note in .beads/issues.jsonl.

files.patch:
@@ -177,7 +177,7 @@
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","title":"Wills & Estate Planning skill launch article","description":"# Wills & Estate Planning skill — launch article + gated install UX

## What this epic covers

Ship a full-length launch article at `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning` that does two things at once:

1. **Promotes** the new `wills-and-estate-planning-skill` on jeffreys-skills.md (the biggest skill yet — 201 files, 572-line SKILL.md, 17 subagents, 45 deliverable templates, 135 reference docs, 8 operating modes, 5 wealth tiers, 11 axioms, 12 cognitive operators, 9 intake phases). Target audience is explicitly **non-technical** — office workers, parents, entrepreneurs, retirees — not just developers.
2. **Explains** the simplest-possible non-terminal install flow: paste a signed agent prompt into Claude Desktop or Codex Desktop, and the agent does the rest. Leverages the paid-jsm.md session in the users browser to gate bundle downloads via short-lived single-use codes. Mirrors the existing `jsm login` auth pattern.

## Why this matters

The tax-preparation skill generated real user demand and real savings ($1k+ per user, with one user saving $20k). An X post teasing the wills skill got strong engagement. But these skills can only become mainstream if *non-developers* can install them. Todays jsm.md install UX requires `jsm install <slug>` in a terminal, which excludes 90% of the target market.

The article serves a secondary purpose: its a **case study in agent-friendly UX**. The paste-a-prompt-into-an-agent pattern generalizes to any paid skill on jsm.md, not just wills, so the article doubles as a template for future non-technical launches.

## Architectural pillars

- **No public GitHub repo** for skill content. Everything gated behind jsm.md auth.
- **Short-lived single-use install codes** minted server-side, bound to `(user_id, skill_id, exp, consumed_at)`.
- **Agent-driven install**: the gated bundle URL is embedded in a copy-paste prompt that the users Claude/Codex agent executes. One tool-use approval on the desktop, no terminal keystrokes from the user.
- **Works on every OS** (macOS / Windows / Linux) because the agent handles path/unzip — no native helper, no code signing required.
- **Bundle watermark** inside the ZIPs README with user_id + timestamp. Deterrent against casual leaks; does not prevent determined piracy (acknowledged trade-off).

## Cross-repo scope

- **This repo (`jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site`)**: the article + hero illustration + 5 custom visualizations + the agent-readable primer markdown in `/public/` + OG images + writing-index registration + command-palette entry + Playwright test.
- **`/dp/jeffreys-skills.md`** (separate bead graph): DB migration + two endpoints + install-card UI + generic non-technical-user guide page. Tracked in that repos .beads/.

## Acceptance criteria

- Article live at `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning` with hero, TOC, 8+ long-form sections, 5 interactive visualizations, OG + Twitter images, and the tri-tab install section.
- Primer markdown downloadable from the article.
- Article visible in the writing index + command palette.
- `bun run build` green, `bun tsc --noEmit` green, `bun lint` green, `ubs` green on changed files.
- Playwright e2e smoke test covers: article renders, TOC links jump, visualizations mount, install-tab switches.
- Depends on jsm.md beads being shipped for the install prompt URLs to resolve; acceptable to ship article first with a "coming soon" placeholder in the install section if jsm.md side is not ready.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:33:55.236585249Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:33:55.236585249Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","title":"Scaffold article route + metadata (app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/page.tsx)","description":"Create `app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/page.tsx` modeled on `app/writing/slack-mattermost-migration/page.tsx`:

- `export const metadata` with title "Drafting a Serious Estate Plan on a Saturday | Jeffrey Emanuel" (or final chosen title), description, and `alternates.canonical` = `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning`.
- `articleSchema` JSON-LD (@type Article) with headline, datePublished (2026-04-XX on launch), author, publisher, mainEntityOfPage, `about` array including: Estate Planning, Wills, Trusts, AI Agents, Agent Skills, Claude Code, Codex, Probate, Incapacity Planning.
- Default export renders `<JsonLd data={articleSchema} />` and `<WillsEstateArticle />`.

## Why this bead exists
Next.js App Router needs the route stub in place before the component file can be referenced. Keeping it tiny + isolated lets us ship the article component independently.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists with metadata + JsonLd + default export.
- `bun tsc --noEmit` clean (will error until the article component is also scaffolded; that is OK — this bead blocks on the component scaffold bead).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:23.442042493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:49:43.759698946Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:49:43.756818132Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:23.442042493Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:33.700736019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.10","title":"Skill catalog callouts (jsm.md links + what shipped in latest release)","description":"Add the paired-callout block directly under the intro, mirroring the slack articles catalog/release pattern.

## Callout 1: Skill catalog page
Single prominent link card:

- **Eyebrow**: `Skill catalog page · opens in a new tab`
- **Link**: `https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill` (dotted-underline, cyan)
- **Caption**: "Full SKILL.md, live visualization, version history, release notes, and the Install in Claude or Codex Desktop button."
- **Heads up**: "That URL returns a 404 unless youre signed in to a jeffreys-skills.md account with an active subscription. Sign up (or log in) at jeffreys-skills.md/dashboard first. Thats the same account the install flow below uses, so doing it now means the install prompt will already know who you are."

## Callout 2: What shipped in the latest release
Match slacks "What shipped in the latest release" pattern:

- **Eyebrow**: `What shipped in the latest release · April 2026`
- Sections:
  - `v1` **the kernel**: 12 axioms, 14 cognitive operators (Probate-Bypass, Spousal-Rights Check, Beneficiary-Title Coherence, Step-Up-vs-Transfer, Liquidity-at-Death, Incapacity-Transition, Lumpy-Asset Division, Cross-State Domicile, Vulnerable-Beneficiary Filter, Tax-Apportionment, Blended-Family QTIP, Disclaimer Window, Trust-Situs Selection, Basis-Consistency), 5 wealth tiers with complexity overlays.
  - `v1` **the intake engine**: 9 adaptive phases (Orientation → Wealth-Tier Routing), 8 operating modes (new-plan / existing-plan-audit / life-event-delta / urgent-bedside-signing / executor-activation / business-owner-succession / uhnw-restructure / maintenance-review), archetype start packs.
  - `v1` **the output contract**: 2 intake files, 20 analyses, 23 deliverables — 45 structured artifacts in total. The contract is enforced: `plan-validator.py` runs as a backstop.
  - `v1` **17 subagents** (intake-conductor, document-organizer, asset-discovery-auditor, beneficiary-audit, anti-pattern-scanner, tax-analyzer, execution-formalities-router, state-law-verifier, overlay-resolver, fiduciary-bench-builder, implementation-ops-planner, funding-checklist-generator, conflict-prevention-planner, litigation-defense-reviewer, multi-model-validator, deliverables-generator, and a meta coordinator).
  - `v1` **the reference corpus**: 135 markdown docs across methodology, intake, foundations, family-structures, assets, incapacity, advanced-planning, tiers, post-death, legacy-and-logistics, anti-patterns, states, professions, life-events, situations, execution-formalities.
  - `v1` **verification-first discipline**: every recommendation that depends on volatile law (2026 OBBBA $15M exemption, state estate tax thresholds, execution formalities) must be checked against primary sources and logged in `analyses/official-source-log.md`.

## Acceptance criteria
- Looks substantially like the slack articles equivalent block.
- All skill-page URLs point at jeffreys-skills.md.
- Heads-up paragraph about 404-unless-authenticated is present — this prevents confused readers from thinking the site is broken.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:03.276128406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:19:03.874763003Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:19:03.817637697Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.10","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:03.276128406Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.10","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.596037324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","title":"Install section with three tabs (agent paste / jsm CLI / manual ZIP)","description":"The single most important section of the article: the non-technical install flow. This is the payoff of the entire auth-code design.

## Section structure (id="install")
SectionHeader eyebrow="Install" title="Get the skill into your agent in 90 seconds."

Then three labeled tabs/cards (Id lean toward 3 stacked cards on mobile and a tab group on desktop — build it as 3 cards with subtle emphasis on card #1):

### Card 1: "In Claude or Codex Desktop (recommended, no terminal)" — default/emphasized

1. **Short pitch**: If youre not a developer, this is the only install path you need. You already have Claude Desktop or Codex Desktop installed (if not, link to the download pages). You have a paid jeffreys-skills.md subscription. Thats it.

2. **Step 1**: Visit the skill page on jeffreys-skills.md (link). Make sure youre signed in.

3. **Step 2**: Click `Generate install prompt`. The site mints a one-time, 10-minute-TTL, single-use code tied to your account and gives you a small block of text to copy.

4. **Step 3**: Open Claude or Codex Desktop. Paste the text. The agent reads it, asks you once to approve the single download tool call (standard "allow this command?" dialog), downloads the bundle, extracts it to the right folder, and tells you to reload skills.

5. **Step 4**: The skill appears in the skill picker. You type `/wills` or just describe what you want (`"I want to review my existing will — I moved from CA to TX last year"`) and the agent routes through the skill.

**Callout**: `Why this works without a terminal` — "The agent IS the terminal. You never type a command; you approve one. The URL embedded in the prompt is a single-use token bound to your paid session, so even if you paste the prompt into someone elses computer it wont work there: the code expires before it can be reused."

### Card 2: "Via the jsm CLI (for developers)"
- Same as slack articles pattern: install jsm, login, `jsm install wills-and-estate-planning-skill`.
- Also mention `claude plugins install` alternative if/when that channel is supported.
- Keep tight — this is the familiar path.

### Card 3: "Manual download (fallback)"
- When the agent-paste flow doesnt work (e.g. the user is running Claude in Chrome or a constrained environment), they can click `Download bundle (.zip)` on the skill page. Same single-use code under the hood.
- Instructions: unzip, then drag `wills-and-estate-planning-skill/` into `~/.claude/skills/` (macOS/Linux) or `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\skills\` (Windows). Same for `~/.codex/skills/` or `~/.agents/skills/` if using Codex.
- Brief mention that theres a "reveal folder" helper button on the skill page that opens Finder/Explorer pointing at the right location.

## Pedagogical aside
After card 3, a short explanatory paragraph about the auth-code flow: the signed URL is how jeffreys-skills.md proves to itself that the paid subscriber clicked the button. Its the same pattern as `jsm login` that developers already know, just re-aimed at install.

## Dependency / coordination
This sections copy should mention specific URLs like `jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill`. The jsm.md-side beads need to have the install-card UI live before this sections instructions are technically accurate. Acceptable to ship the article with the UI partially live and update in a follow-up PR.

## Acceptance criteria
- Three cards render correctly on mobile and desktop.
- Each card has a clear CTA pointing at jsm.md.
- No mock code blocks that imply the user must type bash — the agent does everything.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:30.447308428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:57:07.466443691Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:30.447308428Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:16.260450848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.714423898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: Two strengthenings needed.
(1) The article's install-section copy must match the actual text that appears on the jsm.md install card exactly (button label, TTL wording, generated-code format). If the article says 'click Generate install prompt' and the UI button says 'Get install prompt', readers will think the site is broken. Cross-check against bd-3t7zd.8 at implementation time.
(2) Include explicit copy about failure modes: 'If the code expires before you paste it, go back and click Generate again.' / 'If the agent refuses the download step, click Approve.' / 'If the skill does not appear in the picker after reload, quit and reopen the app once.' Non-technical readers will hit every one of these at least some of the time; leaving them to figure it out by themselves erodes trust in the product.","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:57:07Z"}]}

- Three cards render correctly on mobile and desktop.
- Each card has a clear CTA pointing at jsm.md.
- No mock code blocks that imply the user must type bash — the agent does everything.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:30.447308428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T19:35:12.826355588Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:30.447308428Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:16.260450848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.714423898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: Two strengthenings needed.

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","title":"'Who this is for' section with tier-triage visualization","description":"Address the question every reader has after the install section: "does this apply to me?"

## Section structure (id="who")
SectionHeader eyebrow="Scope" title="Who this is for (and who it isnt)."

### Opening paragraph
Most people think estate planning is for rich people. It is not. Its for anyone who owns anything, cares about anyone, or would prefer a county court not decide what happens to their kids. The skill scales from Tier 1 (renter, mid-20s, no dependents) through Tier 5 ($100M+ industrialist with a private foundation), and the same interview adapts itself to whichever tier matches your facts.

### Embed the TierTriageViz visualization
(Visualization implemented in a separate bead — `wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.)

Interactive: five rungs of a wealth ladder, each with:
- Net worth band
- Archetype ("young-family renter", "suburban homeowner + 401(k)", "founder with RSUs + SLAT candidate", "industrialist / dynasty")
- Primary goals (guardianship, probate avoidance, transfer tax minimization, family governance)
- Core deliverables (will, POAs, beneficiary audit → revocable trust + QTIP → GRAT + IDGT + dynasty trust, etc.)

Tapping a rung expands an inline detail panel. Default selection should be Tier 2 (middle-class homeowner), since thats the modal reader.

### Complexity overlays
Below the viz, a short list of "what bumps you up a tier regardless of net worth":
- Blended family (+1)
- Non-citizen spouse (+1, QDOT territory)
- Privately held business (+1)
- Disabled or vulnerable heir requiring SNT (+1)
- Multi-state or multi-country assets (+1)
- Specialty items: firearms (NFA trust), crypto self-custody, creator-economy IP, pre-IPO stock, addiction/capacity concerns.

### Who this is NOT for (honest scope limits)
- Non-U.S. domiciliaries. The skill is U.S. federal + state + local; foreign-asset overlays exist but the core is U.S. law.
- People who need a will *today* (bedside signing). The skill has an `urgent-bedside-signing` mode, but for true emergencies you want a lawyer on the phone, not an agent.
- People who want a single-page "just write my will" interaction. The skill is deliberately thorough — it produces 45 artifacts — because coherence across documents is the whole point. If you just want a $30 LegalZoom will, go buy one.

## Acceptance criteria
- Visualization renders and is keyboard-accessible.
- Scope limits are explicit — reduces complaints post-launch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:52.573101216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:24:29.193853884Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:24:29.190773721Z","close_reason":"Who-this-is-for section fully implemented in wills-estate-article.tsx lines 886-1007 with TierTriageViz wired in at line 905","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:52.573101216Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.384369815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.838960023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","title":"Core insight section: Axiom 0 + Axiom 1, one coherent story","description":"The thesis section. This is the part that, if the reader leaves with nothing else, should still change how they think about estate planning.

## Section structure (id="insight")
SectionHeader eyebrow="The thesis" title="Why a will alone is not the plan."

### Narrative beats

1. **The most common failure mode is not a drafting error; it is incoherence across documents.** A beautifully-drafted will that says "everything to my kids" does nothing if the 401(k) still names an ex-spouse, because retirement accounts pass by *contract*, not by will. Axiom 0: the will does not control the whole estate.

2. **The plan is the whole story.** Will + revocable trust + beneficiary forms + deeds + POAs + healthcare directives + letter of instruction + digital inventory must all point at the same people, in the same shares, under the same contingencies, under the same states law. Axiom 1: one coherent story.

3. **Concrete example (drive it home)**: "Imagine you die tonight. Your spouse has to locate and call eight institutions in seven days: your 401(k) administrator, two IRA custodians, your life-insurance company, your countys register of deeds, your bank, your brokerage, and the guardian you named for your kids. Each of them will ask a different question. Each of them will accept a different document as proof. The skills job is to make sure the answer they get, and the document you produced, match — so your spouse doesnt spend six months learning probate law at the worst possible time."

### Embed the AxiomCoherenceViz visualization
An interactive SVG/D3 diagram showing:
- Center node: "You"
- 8 spokes out to asset/decision categories (house, 401k, IRA, life insurance, brokerage, POAs, healthcare, digital).
- Each spoke shows which document controls that asset (will / trust / beneficiary form / titling / POA / etc.).
- Toggle "incoherence mode" that highlights mismatches — e.g., a will saying "to kids" but the 401(k) still naming the ex-spouse turns that spoke red.
- Interactive: click a spoke, read a sentence-long plain-English explanation.

### Reinforce with 3 more axioms
- **Axiom 2**: Plan for incapacity first, death second. Dementia, stroke, and coma damage more wealth than death, and lasts longer.
- **Axiom 5**: Under the $15M exemption (2026 OBBBA), basis often dominates tax. Most families are better off *not* gifting appreciated assets out of the estate.
- **Axiom 10**: Communication is the actual work. Surprise at the reading of the will is one of the fastest ways to turn a plan into litigation.

(The other 7 axioms live in the primer and SKILL.md — dont try to cover all 11 here; the article is not a replacement for the skill.)

## Tone discipline
- Every technical term should have a `<J>` tooltip.
- Use names and stakes. Not "elective share." "The rule that says your spouse can override your will and take roughly a third no matter what your will says."
- No code.

## Acceptance criteria
- Visualization mounts, works on touch devices, respects reduced-motion.
- At least one reader test (a non-technical friend/family member) should read this section and be able to articulate "the will is not the plan" back to you.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:16.997426357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:39:01.128246134Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:39:01.125432706Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:16.997426357Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.523855615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.961771157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","title":"Sample session walkthrough (blended-family HNW archetype with IntakePhasesViz)","description":"The emotional centerpiece. A narrated sample session that makes the 9 intake phases concrete.

## Archetype chosen
A **Tier 3 HNW blended family** — because it hits the most instructive patterns in one session:
- Second marriage (both spouses have first-marriage kids)
- One adult child from first marriage is in active addiction recovery
- Primary residence in NY (state estate tax!), vacation home in FL
- One spouse has a pre-IPO startup stake (concentrated position + liquidity risk)
- Family parrot (longer life expectancy than many humans — ties back to the hero illustration and the X-post hook)
- Modest crypto self-custody
- Previous will from 2018 (pre-OBBBA, pre-Secure Act, pre-remarriage)

This archetype cleanly triggers: elective-share, QTIP, vulnerable-heir SNT or incentive trust, NY-FL domicile analysis, liquidity-at-death operator, digital-asset inventory, pet-trust.

## Section structure (id="intake")
SectionHeader eyebrow="A sample session" title="What it actually feels like."

### Presentation
- Narrated from the users perspective ("Maya, 54, remarrying for the second time").
- 9 phases laid out as scenes. For each phase, show:
  - The agents conversational prompt (plain English, not forms).
  - Mayas answer (excerpted / paraphrased).
  - The artifact the agent quietly produces (e.g., `intake-record.md` appended).
  - Any landmine the agent flags as it goes (e.g., "Your ex-husband is still the primary beneficiary on the old 401(k). Flagging `red-flag-triage.md`.").
- After scene 5 (family dynamics), the agent triggers the `blended-family` overlay and the tone of the questions deepens.

### Embed IntakePhasesViz
Visualization showing all 9 phases as a horizontal flow (Orientation → People → Assets → Beneficiary Audit → Family Dynamics → Goals & Values → Incapacity → Jurisdiction → Tier Routing). Each node:
- Clickable — drills into what that phase asks
- Highlight when the narrative is "in" that phase
- Shows approximate time spent (5/10/15 min as in SKILL.md)

### Close the scene
- End with Maya getting a Comprehensive Plan Report, an Attorney Engagement Brief, and a 30-item Implementation Ledger — all produced across a weekend, rather than six months of attorney email threads.
- Crucial line: "The attorney she eventually hires doesnt look at this packet and say a robot did your planning. She looks at it and says good — youve done my diligence work. I can draft in three hours instead of fifteen, and Ill charge you for three."

## Why this archetype
The slack articles emotional centerpiece was the cutover moment. The wills article needs an equivalent — and the blended-family + addicted-child + parrot + crypto combo is vivid enough to stick. Avoid making the archetype so rich that no reader identifies with it; keep one or two elements (the remarriage, the kids) universal.

## Acceptance criteria
- Reads like narrative, not documentation.
- Every `<J>` tooltip works.
- IntakePhasesViz phase-linking animates smoothly as the reader scrolls through the scenes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:42.043211696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:43:45.306097884Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:43:45.302993191Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:42.043211696Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.657149425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:13.086423399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":5,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: IntakePhasesViz scroll-linking (hgjp.23) needs DOM hooks on narrative paragraphs. Mark each scene with data-intake-phase='N' (1..9) on its outer element. Also: the 'Maya' archetype is a Tier 3 HNW blended family — the viz must render the right phase headline for phase 5 (Family Dynamics) when the reader enters that scene, not the default.","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:57:15Z"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","title":"Final build + commit + push (article-side)","description":"Final gate before the article goes live.

## Sequence
1. `bun run build` — zero errors.
2. `bun tsc --noEmit` — clean.
3. `bun lint` — clean.
4. `ubs <changed-files>` — exit 0.
5. `bunx playwright test` — existing suites + new wills-article smoke test all pass.
6. Visual QA on the Vercel preview deploy (one human pass through the article at desktop + mobile viewports).
7. `br sync --flush-only` then `git add .beads/ content/ components/ app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/ lib/ public/ assets/ && git commit` with a message like: "feat(writing): launch wills-and-estate-planning article with 5 visualizations + gated agent-install flow".
8. Push to `main` (Vercel auto-deploys).
9. Verify production URL renders correctly.
10. Verify OG card renders when the URL is pasted into Twitter/X, Slack, LinkedIn.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article live at https://jeffreyemanuel.com/writing/wills-and-estate-planning.
- OG card renders correctly on at least Twitter/X and Slack.
- No production console te-hgjp.36","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.926890955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:54.335231485Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.869990067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.31","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:17.418534035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","title":"Cross-repo coordination note: article install section ↔ jsm.md install UI","description":"# Cross-repo coordination note

## The dependency that crosses repo boundaries
- `jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11` (article install section) references live URLs on jeffreys-skills.md: the skill detail page, the "Generate install prompt" button, etc.
- `bd-3t7zd.19` (jsm.md production deploy) is what makes those URLs real.

`br` does not natively track cross-repo dependencies. This bead exists so that whoever is working on the article remembers to either:
1. **Ship the article AFTER the jsm.md production deploy lands** — simplest, and the URLs in the install section will work on day one.
2. **Ship the article first with "coming soon" framing in the install section** — and file a follow-up bead to revise the article the day jsm.md goes live. Acceptable only if the jsm.md timeline slips.

## Coordination checklist (before pushing the article)
- [ ] `bd-3t7zd.19` (jsm.md prod deploy) is closed OR the articles install section is temporarily framed as "coming soon this week."
- [ ] The skill page URL in the article resolves: `curl -I https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill` returns 200 (or 302 → login; either is fine).
- [ ] The install-card on jsm.md is live and functional end-to-end — one of us has successfully installed the skill via the paste flow at least once on staging or prod.

## Acceptance criteria
- This bead stays open until the article ships with working (not placeholder) install URLs.
- Close with a comment noting which of the two framings was used at ship time.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T19:18:41.720331408Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T19:18:41.717193907Z","close_reason":"Documented the personal-site ↔ jsm.md install contract, clarified unsigned-state wording expectations, and recorded the repo-side validation note in beads.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:12.241720160Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","author":"ubuntu","text":"Cross-repo contract note (2026-04-19)

- Personal-site side owns the article install copy, anchors, and any embedded screenshots or screencasts.
- jsm.md side owns the live skill detail page, the authenticated "Generate install prompt" flow, and the actual install UX after sign-in.
- This article must describe the unsigned experience in user-visible terms ("shows a page-not-found screen"), not as a literal HTTP status promise, because the current unsigned shell renders a page-not-found body behind HTTP 200.
- Any jsm.md install-flow UI change that alters copy, button labels, or step order requires a same-window article update here.
- Repo-local validation in this cycle: direct skill URL resolves, article copy matches unsigned-state behavior, and the exact Chromium TOC rerun passed (`rch exec -- bunx playwright test tests/e2e/wills-estate-article.spec.ts:149 --project=chromium`).","created_at":"2026-04-19T19:18:41Z"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","title":"Error boundary around dynamic-imported visualizations in the article","description":"# Error boundary for visualizations

The article uses Next dynamic({ ssr: false }) to load five visualizations (TierTriageViz, AxiomCoherenceViz, IntakePhasesViz, DeliverablesTreeViz, AntiPatternCardsViz). Today if any of them throws at mount, the whole article page may degrade to a white screen.

## Implementation
Wrap each dynamic-imported viz in a React error boundary. Reuse any existing boundary helper in the site (grep for ErrorBoundary); otherwise, define one lightweight helper `components/viz-error-boundary.tsx` with:

- `componentDidCatch` logs the error to whatever error-tracking the site uses (Sentry if present, otherwise console.error with a stable prefix `[wep-viz-error]`).
- Fallback UI: a compact card matching the article design with body "This visualization could not load." and a "Retry" button that re-mounts the wrapped component. Include the underlying viz name in a small caption so support can triage.
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion on the fallback.
- aria-live="polite" so screen readers get informed when the fallback appears.

## Tests
Playwright scenario: force one viz to throw via a test-only `?break=tier-triage` query param recognized by the viz scaffold bead (hgjp.20). Assert:
- The rest of the article still renders.
- The fallback card appears where the broken viz was.
- Clicking Retry actually re-mounts the viz (send a second `?break=` that disables break-mode on the retry).
- Error logged to console with the expected prefix.

## Acceptance criteria
- Every dynamic-imported viz wrapped.
- Playwright test covers the forced-break scenario.
- No regression in happy-path viz render.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:23.754267463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:23:23.727670596Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:23:23.723607834Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in components/viz-error-boundary.tsx - dynamic imports with SSR disabled + ErrorBoundary wrapper for all 5 viz components","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:23.754267463Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.33","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:04.467669089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","title":"Content QA + proofreading pass before publish","description":"# Content QA + proofreading pass

Explicit human-review gate before the article goes live. The article is launching a paid product to a non-technical audience; typos and confusing copy directly translate to lost subscriptions.

## Review checklist
- [ ] Read the article end-to-end once on desktop, once on mobile.
- [ ] Every claim about the skill ("17 subagents", "45 deliverables", "8 operating modes", "9 intake phases", "11 axioms", "14 operators", "135 reference files", ~$15M 2026 exemption, OBBBA reference, etc.) is factually correct against the current SKILL.md.
- [ ] Every URL to jsm.md resolves — no 404s.
- [ ] Every `<J>` jargon tooltip renders a plain-English definition; no "?? undefined" in prod.
- [ ] Every section heading anchor (`#install`, `#who`, `#insight`, etc.) links from the TOC correctly.
- [ ] No typos, inconsistent capitalization, or duplicated sentences. Run the article copy through a proofing pass.
- [ ] Disclaimers (not-legal-advice, attorney-handoff) are present and prominent.
- [ ] No implied guarantees ("you will save $X") — frame as "readers have saved between $X and $Y" with the X-post framing.
- [ ] Author byline + publish date visible.
- [ ] OG card renders correctly when pasted into Twitter/X, Slack, LinkedIn, iMessage.
- [ ] Read the primer markdown (hgjp.6) in full — is it internally consistent? Does it pair with the article content?
- [ ] Walk one non-technical friend through the article (not a developer) and observe where they stumble — log each stumble as a follow-up bead.

## Outcome
A short "proof of review" comment on this bead noting who reviewed, what they fixed, and any follow-ups filed.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article passes the end-to-end read aloud without the reviewer flinching.
- At least one non-dev has walked through the install section and successfully pasted the prompt into Claude/Codex (can pair with bd-3t7zd.18 staging verify).
- No open P1 typo/copy bugs on the article.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:40.864491627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.426523850Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:40.864491627Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:04.675163979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:04.780711951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:04.889812917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:04.997058168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:05.104000622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:05.216479726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:05.328990739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_s +{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.34","title":"Content QA + proofreading pass before publish","description":"# Content QA + proofreading pass

Explicit human-review gate before the article goes live. The article is launching a paid product to a non-technical audience; typos and confusing copy directly translate to lost subscriptions.

## Review checklist
- [ ] Read the article end-to-end once on desktop, once on mobile.
- [ ] Every claim about the skill ("17 subagents", "45 deliverables", "8 operating modes", "9 intake phases", "11 axioms", "14 operators", "135 reference files", ~$15M 2026 exemption, OBBBA reference, etc.) is factually correct against the current SKILL.md.
- [ ] Every URL to jsm.md resolves — no 404s.
- [ ] Every `<J>` jargon tooltip renders a plain-English definition; no "?? undefined" in prod.
- [ ] Every section heading anchor (`#install`, `#who`, `#insight`, etc.) links from the TOC correctly.
- [ ] No typos, inconsistent capitalization, or duplicated sentences. Run the article copy through a proofing pass.
- [ ] Disclaimers (not-legal-advice, attorney-handoff) are present and prominent.
- [ ] No implied guarantees ("you will save $X") — frame as "readers have saved between $X and $Y" with the X-post framing.
- [ ] Author byline + publish date visible.
- [ ] OG card renders correctly when pasted into Twitter/X, Slack, LinkedIn, iMessage.
- [ ] Read the primer markdown (hgjp.6) in full — is it internally consistent? Does it pair with the article content?
- [ ] Walk one non-technical friend through the article (not a developer) and observe where they stumble — log each stumble as a follow-up bead.

## Outcome
A short "proof of review" comment on this bead noting who reviewed, what they fixed, and any follow-ups filed.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article passes the end-to-end read aloud without the reviewer flinching.
- At least one non-dev has walked through the install section and successfully pasted the prompt into Claude/Codex (can pair with bd-3t7zd.18 staging verify).
- No open P1 typo/copy bugs on the  {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","title":"Sitemap + RSS + social-preview entries for the new article","description":"# Sitemap + RSS update

Make sure the new article is indexed everywhere the site indexes writing:

## Checks
- [ ] `/sitemap.xml` (check `app/sitemap.ts` or equivalent) includes `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning` with the right `lastmod`.
- [ ] `/robots.txt` does not disallow the new path.
- [ ] RSS feed (if the site has one — grep for `/rss`, `/feed`, `app/feed.xml/route.ts`) includes the new article.
- [ ] Writing-index page (`/writing`) surfaces the article card.
- [ ] `manifest.ts` + favicon behave correctly on the new route.

## Verification
- `curl -s https://jeffreyemanuel.com/sitemap.xml | grep wills-and-estate-planning` returns a hit.
- Paste URL into https://cards-dev.twitter.com/validator (or the current Twitter/X card validator) and confirm the OG card renders.
- Paste into https://www.linkedin.com/post-inspector/ and verify.

## Acceptance criteria
- Sitemap entry present.
- RSS entry present (if RSS exists on the site).
- Social-preview validators return the expected card without warnings.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:50.222423103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:58:46.893178109Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:58:46.890389466Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:59:50.222423103Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.463439992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.573685101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.35","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.642751177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","title":"Analytics: conversion events on article CTAs","description":"# Analytics events on article CTAs

Knowing which part of the article converts is how we iterate. Zero analytics = flying blind.

## Events to emit
Use whatever analytics the site already has (check `components/` for existing analytics wrappers; probably GA4 or Plausible). Emit:
- `article_viewed` (slug=wills-and-estate-planning, scroll_depth_bucket on unload: 0-25/25-50/50-75/75-100)
- `primer_download_clicked` (from the download CTA section)
- `install_section_cta_clicked` (target: "claude" | "codex" | "cli" | "manual-zip" — depending on which card the user clicks)
- `jsm_skill_page_outbound_clicked` (target: "wills-and-estate-planning-skill" or whichever URL was clicked)
- `toc_link_clicked` (anchor id)
- `visualization_interacted` (viz_name, action: "tier_selected" | "axiom_toggled" | "phase_drilled" | "deliverable_opened" | "antipattern_flipped")

All events include: referrer, session_id, article_slug, viewport_class ("mobile" | "tablet" | "desktop"). PII-free — no user_id (the article is public, so there IS no authenticated user on this page).

## Implementation
Define a tiny `emitEvent(name, props)` helper in `lib/analytics.ts` (or extend existing) if not already present. The article component calls it on the listed interactions.

## Privacy
Honour Do-Not-Track + the sites cookie-consent posture. If the site already has an analytics layer with opt-out wiring, inherit it automatically.

## Acceptance criteria
- Events observable in the analytics dashboard within 24h of deploy.
- No PII in any event.
- Dev-mode (NODE_ENV=development) can log events to console instead of sending, for debugging.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:00:04.316751104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:26:12.102826780Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:00:04.316751104Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.36","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:03:06.812792118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.37","title":"FAQ section at end of article (anticipates common reader blockers)","description":"# FAQ section

Non-technical readers arrive with specific, predictable doubts. A FAQ section just before the TL;DR answers them inline rather than forcing them to email support or just abandon.

## Section structure (new id="faq" — add to TOC in bead .9)
SectionHeader eyebrow="FAQ" title="Questions you are about to ask."

## Question set (minimum — expand to ~15 during writing)
1. **Is this legal advice?** No — the skill produces audit + drafts + handoff packets. Your attorney signs.
2. **Do I still need an attorney?** Yes, for signing + state-specific formalities. The skill makes the attorneys job smaller, not optional. (Cross-reference section .18.)
3. **Is my data private?** The agent runs on your computer, not ours. The skill never transmits your financial documents anywhere — it reads local files and writes local files.
4. **What if Im not in the U.S.?** The skill is U.S.-only today. Foreign-asset overlays exist for U.S. domiciliaries with assets abroad, but if you are domiciled outside the U.S., this is not for you yet.
5. **Will the skill work if tax law changes?** The skill separates evergreen methodology from volatile law. Every live-law check is verified against primary sources and logged in `official-source-log.md`.
6. **Why $20/month instead of $20 once?** The skill is a living system — law updates, improvements, new subagents — that accrues value over time. Cancel anytime.
7. **What if I only need a simple will?** Youre probably better served by LegalZoom or Trust & Will. The skills value is in coordination across 8+ document types; if you just need one document, its overkill.
8. **How is this different from LegalZoom / Trust & Will / Rocket Lawyer?** (Link to the comparison section or paragraph — separate bead .38 if we build a full table.)
9. **Can I share the skill with my spouse / partner?** One subscription per user. Your spouse needs their own. The watermark on every bundle is tied to your user ID.
10. **What happens when I cancel?** You keep anything youve already produced — those are your files on your computer. You lose access to future updates + re-download. If you cancel mid-planning, you can finish offline with whats already on disk.
11. **Does it work on my phone?** Claude Code Desktop and Codex Desktop are Mac/Windows/Linux — no mobile app today. You need a computer.
12. **Can my attorney see what the skill produced?** Yes — the Attorney Engagement Brief is designed for exactly that. Take it to your lawyer; they will thank you.
13. **What if I get partway through and lose interest?** Your `intake-record.md` persists. Come back in a month, three months, or a year. The skill has a `maintenance-review` mode for periodic refresh.
14. **Is my crypto safe? Youre asking me about my seed phrase?** The skill NEVER asks for your seed phrase. The `digital-inventory.md` template has an explicit field that says "pointer to where your seed phrase is stored — do NOT put the seed phrase itself here."
15. **How long does this take?** A first full pass is typically 3-6 hours spread across a weekend, not a single sitting. Updates after life events take 30-60 min.

## Placement
Between the "Attorney handoff" section (.18) and the "TL;DR / pattern" section (.19). Add to the TOC anchor list.

## Accessibility
Each Q/A is a `<details>` element with `<summary>` for the question — native keyboard + screen-reader support, no JS needed.

## Acceptance criteria
- FAQ renders between .18 and .19.
- TOC includes the FAQ anchor.
- 15+ questions answered.
- Every Q has a plain-English A under 120 words.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:08:28.475969991Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:33:56.428453648Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:33:56.425616927Z","close_reason":"Code complete, verified by IvoryOrchid","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.37","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:08:28.475969991Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.37","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:53.443371209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

+```powershell

+
+# Log in
+jsm login
+
+# Install the skill

+```
+
+If Claude or Codex is already open, reload the skill picker (or restart the app) after installation.

 The skill installs into `~/.claude/skills/` and `~/.codex/skills/` where Claude Code and Codex automatically discover it.
